body_txt: When you introduce yourself to someone by name, sometimes you have to literally spell it out for them. It's not uncommon for some characters to need to spell out their names if it is fairly unconventional (such as because of cultural translations or just matters of individuality ), but then you have these characters, who do it at virtually every available opportunity, likely in ways that are completely random and unsolicited. Most times, this gets played as a lighthearted character quirk that can bring some laughs, especially if has a particularly long name that they spell out slowly . Note that this isn't limited to English, and can apply to any language with a writing system where names can be written "incorrectly" and require distinctions. Subtrope of Always Introduces Themselves , compare with Spelling for Emphasis . See also Pronouncing My Name for You , when it's about a character explaining how to correctly speak their name.
